summary: Turned Path Map Node Intel related events into a clickable master-detail proof lens so each selected route event exposes status, time, artifact, and summary without leaving the board.

queries: nested drill down activity feed detail panel UX pattern
master detail activity timeline dashboard event detail UX
progressive disclosure timeline event details dashboard UI
pattern_notes: Master-detail keeps the event list and selected record visible together, avoiding route-context loss.
Progressive disclosure reveals proof details only after the operator selects an event, keeping the first scan compact.
Drilldown navigation patterns favor stable selected states and clear return context for nested records.

data_inputs: focused Path Map node related events
ui_contract: Create deterministic event keys from existing trail fields without adding snapshot schema.
Render related event rows as focusable buttons with aria-pressed selected state.
Default the Event Proof lens to the first related event for the focused node.
Let a clicked related event update the proof lens in browser memory only.
Show event title, status, summary, type, time, and artifact path while preserving the full Path Map context.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, there was no .path-event-lens, no data-path-event-focus buttons, no selected event, and no Event Proof text.

green_route.evidence: The route rendered 3 clickable related events, exactly 1 selected event, a proof grid, and an Event Proof lens. Clicking the second event changed the lens title to Service Request Packet Scaffolded with no console errors, failed requests, or bad rectangles.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports kept 3 event buttons, 1 selected event, the updated proof lens, document width equal to viewport width, and no bad rectangles or request failures.
